title: Trickle timer integer overflow in transmit_time calculation
description:
File: rust/lichen-rpl/src/trickle.rs
Lines: 113-116

The transmit_time calculation does not handle integer overflow:
```rust
let half = self.interval / 2;
let offset = if half > 0 { rand_offset % half } else { 0 };
self.transmit_time = now + half + offset;
```

Impact: If 'now' is close to u32::MAX (approximately 49 days of milliseconds), adding half + offset will cause integer overflow, wrapping around to a small value. This would result in:
1. transmit_time appearing to be in the distant past
2. Immediate DIO transmission instead of waiting
3. Potential flooding of the network with DIOs
4. Trickle algorithm correctness violation

For long-running embedded systems (>49 days uptime), this is a real concern.

Fix: Use saturating_add or implement proper time wrapping semantics:
```rust
self.transmit_time = now.saturating_add(half).saturating_add(offset);
```
close_reason: Fixed: Changed transmit_time calculation to use saturating_add() instead of plain addition, preventing wraparound when now is close to u32::MAX. Added test to verify saturation behavior.
